title: Databricks Makes Spark Easier
summary: Spark's creators founded Databricks to reduce the operational burden of managing infrastructure. It provides managed clusters, serverless compute, collaborative notebooks, and scheduled jobs in the cloud.

context.headline: The engine, wrapped in an easy platform
context.description: Apache Spark was powerful but hard to operate. Databricks, founded by Spark's creators, wraps the engine in a managed cloud platform: start compute with a few actions, use collaborative notebooks, and schedule production jobs without managing servers directly. On Microsoft's cloud, it is offered as a first-party service called Azure Databricks.
context.when: From founding the company to today's serverless, collaborative platform
